task_completion.description: Verify the template file is being created or saved at the exact required path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal command, text editor window, or file explorer showing the path '/opt/geoserver/data_dir/workspaces/ne/postgis_ne/ne_countries/content.ftl'.

task_completion.description: Verify the template is designed to output an HTML table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for HTML table tags such as <table>, <tr>, <th>, and <td> within the file contents.

task_completion.description: Verify the template uses Freemarker syntax to iterate over the features.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Freemarker list directives, typically <#list features as feature> or similar, indicating dynamic processing of the WMS response.

task_completion.description: Verify the template explicitly references the five required attributes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the exact lowercase attribute names in the template variables: 'name', 'iso_a3', 'pop_est', 'economy', and 'continent' (e.g., ${feature.name.value}, ${feature.iso_a3.value}).

task_completion.description: Verify the template is restricted to displaying ONLY the five required attributes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Ensure the template explicitly defines rows/cells for ONLY the 5 required attributes. It should NOT contain a generic loop over all attributes (like <#list feature.attributes as attribute>) unless there is a strict <#if> condition filtering for only the 5 specified keys.

integrity.description: Verify the agent created a dynamic template rather than hardcoding a static response.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file must contain Freemarker variables (e.g., ${...}) and not hardcoded values for a specific country like 'France' or 'FRA'.

integrity.description: Verify the file was actually saved to the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output confirming the file write (e.g., returning to the bash prompt after using nano/vim, or a successful 'cat' or 'echo' command writing to the file).

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just copy the default GeoServer template.
integrity.visual_evidence:
The default GeoServer template iterates over all attributes generically (
  • ${attribute.name}: ${attribute.value}
). The agent's file must be a custom HTML table specifically targeting the 5 requested attributes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct file path is exactly '/opt/geoserver/data_dir/workspaces/ne/postgis_ne/ne_countries/content.ftl'. The template must use lowercase attribute names: 'name', 'iso_a3', 'pop_est', 'economy', and 'continent' because ogr2ogr converts field names to lowercase during PostgreSQL import. The template must be a valid Freemarker template (using <#list> and ${...}) that generates an HTML table, not a static HTML file.
